headline: Bears Win Big as SPY and QQQ Tumble Despite Claude's Bullish Bets
summary: Models were deeply divided on market direction, with GPT-4o and Perplexity correctly calling the bearish turn while Claude went bullish and paid the price. Tesla emerged as the day's consensus short, with all models correctly predicting its decline following earnings warnings. The standout winner was Grok's AAPL call and GPT-4o's QQQ short, while Claude's SPY long suffered the worst loss.

consensus_picks.note: The bulls got crushed as SPY fell to 708.45, with only GPT-4o and Perplexity correctly anticipating the decline.
Universal agreement on Tesla's weakness proved spot-on as it tumbled to 373.72 following capex concerns.
Despite AI investment hype, NVDA disappointed the bulls by closing at 199.64.
Bitcoin bulls were rewarded as crypto climbed to 78151.01 amid market uncertainty.

best_call.summary: GPT-4o nailed the market's bearish turn with high confidence, correctly anticipating SPY's drop amid inflation concerns.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence bullish bet on QQQ backfired spectacularly as tech stocks tumbled instead of rallying.
